words.definition_en: A special celebration with fun activities.
Traditional dancing by groups of people.
When two people get married ceremony.
When family members gather together again.
Outdoor event with games and food.
Competition to see who is fastest.
Group of people working together.
Person who comes first in competition.
Points earned in game or competition.
Person who plays game or sport.

examples: The people are excited. The game is exciting.
That race was really tiring.
Races are boring. Pizza is more interesting.
He's always interested in pizza.
rules: Adjectives that end in -ed tell about how we feel.
Adjectives that end in -ing usually tell about a thing, such as a game or a movie.

topic: Sports and Leisure, The World Around Us

vocabulary.topic: Sports and Leisure, The World Around Us

words.definition_en: Hit something with your foot.
A game where one person chases others.
Jump over a rope going round and round.
Jump up and down repeatedly quickly.
Let something fall to the ground.
A large flat area with few trees.
words.definition_zh: 有很少樹的大平坦地區

words.definition_en: A large body of water surrounded by land.
A deep valley with high rocky sides.
words.definition_zh: 有高岩石兩側的深山谷

words.definition_en: A very steep high face of rock.
A small river that flows through land.

examples: Ashanti's friends like skipping.
Maria likes landing on one foot.
Running and jumping are lots of fun!
Dancing to music is definitely fun!
rules: When you add -ing to a verb, it can act like a noun. It's called a gerund.

words.definition_en: Putting things into bags or boxes.
words.definition_zh: 把东西放进袋子或盒子里

words.definition_en: Going to live in a new house.
Saying hello to people who live near you.
words.definition_zh: 和住在你附近的人打招呼

words.definition_en: Buying things you need at a store.
words.definition_zh: 在商店购买你需要的东西

words.definition_en: Sliding on ice wearing special shoes.
words.definition_zh: 穿着特殊鞋子在冰上滑行

words.definition_en: Trying to catch fish in water.
Gathering fallen leaves with a rake tool.
words.definition_zh: 用耙子把落下的叶子聚集起来

words.definition_en: Moving your body to music in rhythm.
words.definition_zh: 随着音乐有节奏地移动你的身体

words.definition_en: Tossing balls of snow at people playfully.
words.definition_zh: 好玩地把雪球扔向别人

words.definition_en: Sliding down snowy hills on a sled.

examples: Sati can ice skate.
Sati can't play with Nisha.
Sati couldn't ice skate.
Sati could play with Nisha.
rules: Can tells us what someone knows how to do now, in the present.
Could tells us what someone knew how to do before, in the past.

words.definition_en: A large land with its own government.
Quiet area near a big city with houses.
Countryside with farms and very few people.
Busy city with many buildings and people.
Big building with many stores inside.
School for adults to learn and study.
Place where sick people get medical help.
Building where machines make products.
Small place where doctors treat sick people.
Place where wild animals live and people visit.

examples: If I'm sleepy, I run to the park.
When another city has good services, people want to move there.
If ice gets warm, it melts.
When I'm hungry, I have a snack.
rules: Something that happens can cause something else to happen.
Use if or when to describe an event that causes a second event.

words.definition_en: Small red insect with black spots.
Jumping insect that makes chirping sounds.
Green jumping insect that eats plants.
Hard-bodied insect with shiny shell.
Green insect that looks like grasshopper.
Insect that makes honey and pollinates flowers.
Colorful insect with four large wings.
Dense group of bushes and small trees.
Large jumping animal from Australia.
Small dried grape that is sweet.

examples: What were they doing last night? They were watching television.
The birds were singing, and the sun was shining.
My dad was frying eggs in the kitchen.
I was eating breakfast with my mom.
rules: The past continuous tells what was going on at a certain time in the past.
Use was or were plus a verb ending in -ing.

words.definition_en: A group of words that tells complete thought.
Words that sound the same at the end.
A word that shows an action or movement.
A word that names a person or thing.
A word that describes how something looks.
One part of a word when you say it.
A symbol we use to write words and sounds.
A small bird with a red breast.
A colorful flat fish that lives in water.
Tiny crystals of ice that fall from sky.

examples: I was walking to school this morning when I saw an orange butterfly.
I was dreaming of the ocean when the rain came down.
I was walking to the circus when I met a funny clown.
I was searching for my sock when I saw my shoe.
rules: You can use the past continuous and the simple past in the same sentence.
The simple past tells us about something that happened. The past continuous tells us what was going on when it happened.

words.definition_en: A very short amount of time.
Sixty seconds of time passing.
Sixty minutes of time passing.
About four weeks of time.
Twelve months or 365 days.
Twelve o'clock in the daytime.
To turn around and around quickly.
To travel in a circle around something.
Areas where clocks show different times.
To add a number many times together.

examples: In two hours, it will be noon where you live.
On the other side of the world it won't be noon. It will be 12:00 at night!
In four hours, it will be two o'clock and I will be in Spain!
We'll go to the beach and swim in the sea.
rules: Use will to talk about future facts.
Remember, a fact is something that is true.

words.definition_en: Place where airplanes take off and land.
Person traveling on a plane or vehicle.
Special book needed to travel to countries.
The place you are going to visit.
Not feeling well or healthy in body.
Pain or hurt inside your ear area.
Pain or hurt in your head area.
When your body temperature gets too hot.
Person who helps sick people feel better.
Special liquid or pills to make you healthy.

examples: My mom is going to give me a present.
I'm going to take care of you all day.
She's going to play again today.
My mom is going to cook tomorrow.
rules: You can use going to to talk about a plan for the future.
The plan was made before the speaker talks about it.

words.definition_en: Machine with blades that spin in the wind.
Boat that uses wind to push it forward.
Tall machine that catches wind to make power.
Many wind turbines together making electricity.
Black rock from ground we burn for energy.
Thick liquid from ground we use for fuel.
Invisible fuel from ground we burn for heat.
Dirty air, water, or soil that harms nature.
Big factory that makes electricity for cities.
Power that lights our homes and runs machines.

examples: We may find other sources of green energy in the future.
Renewable energy might also reduce our need for fossil fuels.
You might see solar panels in your area.
I may get up early and go jogging.
rules: You can use may or might to talk about something when you are not sure it will happen.

words.definition_en: Underground train that moves people around cities.
words.definition_zh: 地下鐵路運送城市裡的人

words.definition_en: Big red truck that puts out fires.
Group shares one car to same place.
words.definition_zh: 一群人共用一輛車去同一地方

words.definition_en: Special vehicle that helps sick or hurt people.
words.definition_zh: 幫助生病或受傷的人的車

words.definition_en: Moving stairs that go up and down.
Long vehicle on tracks carrying many people.
words.definition_zh: 在軌道上運送很多人的長車

words.definition_en: Car you pay to ride to different places.
words.definition_zh: 付錢乘坐去不同地方的車

words.definition_en: Machine that moves people up and down buildings.
words.definition_zh: 在建築物上下移動人的機器

words.definition_en: Aircraft with spinning blades that flies high.
words.definition_zh: 有旋轉葉片會飛的飛行器

words.definition_en: Lots of cars stuck and moving very slowly.

examples: Hector uses a little energy.
There were only a few cars on the road.
Mr. Big uses a lot of energy.
There were lots of cars in the traffic jam.
rules: Use a little to describe a small amount of something you cannot count, such as energy.
Use a few to describe a small amount of something you can count, such as cars.
Use a lot of or lots of to describe a big amount of something we can or cannot count.

words.definition_en: Large stringed instrument played with a bow.
Large drums played with special sticks.
Brass instrument with sliding tube sound.
Tall wooden instrument with deep sound.
Large group of musicians playing together.
Person who leads musicians with baton.
Thin stick used to lead musicians.
Person who travels and works in space.
Person who teaches animals circus tricks.
Person who studies and discovers new things.

examples: This elephant is louder than that one.
That elephant is the loudest of the three elephants.
Being a good friend is more important than those things.
I am the most wonderful friend in the world!
rules: Use comparative adjectives to talk about two things.
Use superlative adjectives to talk about three or more things.
Add -er/-est for short adjectives; use more/most for longer adjectives.

words.definition_en: Double pipe wind instrument from Middle East.
Long thin instrument you blow into.
African drum shaped like a goblet.
Big brass instrument that sounds low.
Instrument made of pipes tied together.
Small stringed instrument played with bow.
Long wooden instrument from Australia.
Drum with wires that makes sharp sound.
Chinese stringed instrument played with bow.
Wooden instrument you blow into gently.

examples: The sound of the didgeridoo is as low as the tuba's sound.
The didgeridoo is not as curved as the tuba.
The drum is as loud as the falling rain.
My pencil isn't as long as yours.
rules: Use as + adjective + as to compare things that are the same in one way.
Use not + as + adjective + as to compare things that are different in one way.

bigQuestion: How do inventions change our lives?

words.definition_en: A bar that helps lift heavy things.
A sloped surface for moving things up.
words.definition_zh: 用來上升移動物品的斜面

words.definition_en: A triangle shape that splits things apart.
words.definition_zh: 三角形物體用來分開東西

words.definition_en: A twisty fastener that holds things together.
words.definition_zh: 旋轉的螺釘用來固定東西

words.definition_en: A round wheel that spins on a rod.
A glass bulb that makes light when on.
A round handle you turn to open doors.
words.definition_zh: 轉動打開門的圓形把手

words.definition_en: A smooth slope you slide down for fun.
words.definition_zh: 光滑的斜坡可以滑下來玩

words.definition_en: A tool with sharp blade for chopping wood.
words.definition_zh: 用來砍木頭的銳刀工具

words.definition_en: A plank that goes up and down with two people.
words.definition_zh: 兩個人可以上下搖動的木板

examples: A hammer is a lever. A seesaw is a lever, too.
The wedge isn't a new invention. The ramp isn't, either.
Harry is careful and Jay is, too.
Harry doesn't fall off and Jay doesn't, either.
rules: Use too to agree with something positive (for example, with is).
Use either to agree with something negative (for example, with isn't).

bigQuestion: How do inventions change our lives?

words.definition_en: Special frames with glass to help you see.
A container used for holding flowers.
A very hot machine that heats things.
A thin metal thread that conducts electricity.
A place where people make things with tools.
The outer part that holds eyeglass lenses.
Clear hard material used in windows.
A person who makes things from hot glass.
Dishes and cups made of glass material.
A container with a handle for pouring liquids.

examples: Antonio made his glassware faster than before.
He ran faster than / the fastest in the race.
Our class walked more quietly than their class.
I get up later than you in the morning.
rules: Use comparative and superlative adverbs to compare the same action.
An adverb describes an action.
Add -er/-est for short adverbs; use more/most for longer adverbs.

words.definition_en: Dirt where plants grow their roots.
Tiny objects that grow into plants.
Plant parts underground that drink water.
The main stalk holding up the plant.
Flat green parts that make plant food.
Colorful parts that make flowers pretty.
Sweet juice inside flowers that bees drink.
Yellow dust that helps make new seeds.
Air gas that all living things need.
People like you and your family.

examples: Fill a pot with a bag of soil. Moisten the soil with a cup of water.
I bought a box of seeds and a kilogram of apples.
I have a big loaf of bread.
A jar of strawberry jam.
rules: Use measure words to count things that we can't count, such as soil and water.
Use measure words to count larger numbers of things we can count, such as seeds and apples.

words.definition_en: Person who grows plants and flowers.
Orange root vegetable that tastes sweet.
Small seeds we eat as healthy food.
Plants with long stems that climb up.
Area where people grow plants and vegetables.
Thick air that makes it hard to see.
Young people between thirteen and nineteen.
Group of people living in same area.
Large group of people standing together.
Wheeled basket for carrying store items.

examples: Marisol is curious about her plant.
She was surprised at the big sunflower.
She was very happy with the mural.
I'm curious about rain forests.
rules: Adjectives with prepositions tell how we feel about something.

words.definition_en: Land surrounded by water on all sides.
When air moves fast around you.
Water falling down from high place.
White fluffy things floating in sky.
Small balls of ice falling from sky.
Place where land meets the ocean.
Thick cloud near ground making hard to see.
Very bad weather with wind and rain.
Bright flash of light during thunderstorm.
Long stream of water flowing to ocean.

examples: Go up to the deck and check the sails.
We sailed down the coast of South America.
Walk down this street, through the park, and around the pond.
He went out of bed and into the kitchen.
rules: Use prepositions of movement to give directions.

words.definition_en: Special glasses that protect your eyes.
Big seaweed that grows in the ocean.
Black and white bird that swims very well.
Ocean animal with ten long arms.
Furry animal that swims in water.
Colorful rock-like animal in the ocean.
Huge ocean animal with a bumpy back.
Smart ocean animal that jumps high.
Ocean animal with leaf-like body parts.
Sea creature with eight arms and no bones.

grammar.point: Want to / Need to / Have to
examples: Cousteau wanted to stay underwater longer.
He needed to (had to) use the Aqua-Lung to stay underwater.
I want to swim at a penguin's side.
I need to breathe.
rules: Use want to when you have a choice to do something.
Use need to or have to when you don't have a choice. You must do it.
